name: spurious_self_collision_bits

symptoms: the arm cannot reach or hold a commanded configuration although the IK solution is kinematically perfect
large steady-state error on PROXIMAL joints (shoulder ~0.7 rad) while distal joints track fine
physics 'explosions' (PhysX Illegal BroadPhaseUpdateData) when rigidly-attached parts overlap
trace_evidence: kinematic teleport to the IK solution -> TCP within ~1 mm; running physics on the SAME config -> TCP 150 mm off
contact pairs between links that are rigidly related or must legitimately pass close: arm_link <-> its mounting bracket / lift column / grafted gripper (impulse 8-33)

when_to_apply: SolidWorks/CAD-derived robots whose convex collision hulls over-approximate, causing contacts between links that can never truly collide (rigid subassemblies) or that must operate in close proximity by design.
repair_strategy: Give every link of the rigidly-related subtree the SAME collision-group-2 bit (SAPIEN: pairs sharing a group-2 bit are ignored). Include easy-to-miss members: the arm's mounting BRACKET and the shared lift COLUMN, not just the serial-chain links. Keep object/table collision intact. Verify with a teleport+hold test: joint error should collapse (0.7 rad -> <0.1).
code_sketch: group = ['vertical_link', f'{side}_base'] + [f'{side}_link{i}' for i in range(1, 5)]
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro: TCP error 152 mm -> 17 mm after adding left_base+vertical_link to the bit group; grasp then succeeded
